creator: jfc@mit.edu
text: I probably did want poweroff this time.  I learned Unix when you had to flip the power switch by hand.  What's the dominant configuration of FreeBSD: desktop with USB keyboard, server with USB keyboard, or server with builtin serial port driven by IPMI?  Other configurations with a serial console exist but I predict they are uncommon.  Is it likely that somebody will have an input source connected to a builtin 16550 UART after a USB keyboard has been detached?
